meta.geometry_note: All sections MCQ only. Questions are about construction conditions, properties, and measurements ‚ÄĒ no actual drawing required.

questions.text: To construct a unique quadrilateral, the minimum number of measurements required is:
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral has 4 sides, 4 angles and 2 diagonals = 10 measurements total. To construct a unique quadrilateral, we need exactly 5 independent measurements.

questions.text: A quadrilateral ABCD can be constructed if we are given:
questions.explanation: 4 sides + 1 diagonal = 5 measurements ‚Üí unique quadrilateral. Only 4 sides is not enough (multiple quadrilaterals possible). Only 4 angles is not enough (size not fixed).

questions.text: In constructing quadrilateral ABCD with 4 sides and 1 diagonal AC, the diagonal divides it into:

options.b: Two triangles that can each be constructed

questions.explanation: Diagonal AC divides ABCD into triangles ABC and ACD. Given 4 sides and diagonal AC, each triangle has 3 known sides ‚Üí can be uniquely constructed by SSS.

questions.text: Which set of measurements is sufficient to construct quadrilateral PQRS uniquely?

options.a: PQ, QR, RS, SP (four sides only)

questions.explanation: 3 sides + 2 included angles = 5 measurements ‚Üí unique quadrilateral. Four sides alone are insufficient.

questions.text: To construct a square of side 5 cm, how many measurements are needed?
questions.explanation: A square has all sides equal and all angles 90¬į. Knowing just 1 side length (5 cm) is enough to construct it uniquely.

questions.text: In the figure, quadrilateral ABCD is to be constructed with AB = 4 cm, BC = 5 cm, CD = 6 cm, DA = 3 cm and diagonal AC = 7 cm. Which triangle is constructed first?

options.c: Either triangle can be constructed first

questions.explanation: With diagonal AC known, both ‚Ė≥ABC (sides AB, BC, AC) and ‚Ė≥ACD (sides AC, CD, DA) have all 3 sides known. Either can be constructed first.

questions.text: A quadrilateral ABCD is given with 3 sides and 2 diagonals. The diagonals are used to:

options.c: Determine if it is a parallelogram

questions.explanation: With 3 sides and 2 diagonals, the two diagonals intersect to locate the 4th vertex of the quadrilateral.

questions.text: For constructing a rhombus, the minimum information needed is:
questions.explanation: A rhombus has all 4 sides equal. So 1 side gives all sides. But we still need an angle (or diagonal) to fix the shape uniquely. 1 side + 1 angle = 2 measurements is sufficient for a rhombus.

questions.text: In constructing quadrilateral MORE with MO = 6 cm, OR = 4.5 cm, ‚ą†M = 60¬į, ‚ą†O = 105¬į and ‚ą†R = 105¬į. What is ‚ą†E?
questions.explanation: Sum of angles of quadrilateral = 360¬į. ‚ą†E = 360¬į ‚ąí 60¬į ‚ąí 105¬į ‚ąí 105¬į = 90¬į.

questions.text: When constructing a quadrilateral given 2 adjacent sides and 3 angles, which condition must hold?

options.b: Sum of all 4 angles must be 360¬į
options.c: The 3 angles must all be equal
options.d: Sum of 2 adjacent angles must be 180¬į

questions.explanation: Sum of all 4 interior angles of a quadrilateral = 360¬į. The 4th angle = 360¬į ‚ąí sum of the 3 given angles.

questions.text: To construct a rectangle with length 6 cm and breadth 4 cm, we use the property that:
questions.explanation: The defining property used in constructing a rectangle is that each interior angle = 90¬į. We draw perpendicular sides at each vertex.

questions.text: In the figure, quadrilateral ABCD is constructed with AB = 4.5 cm, BC = 5.5 cm, CD = 4 cm, AD = 6 cm and ‚ą†B = 75¬į. The construction uses which method?
questions.explanation: 4 sides (AB, BC, CD, AD) + 1 angle (‚ą†B) = 5 measurements. This corresponds to '3 sides and 2 angles' method where ‚ą†B is the included angle between AB and BC.

questions.text: A quadrilateral cannot be constructed if the given diagonal is longer than:

options.a: The sum of any two given sides

questions.explanation: A triangle is constructible only when each side < sum of other two sides. If the diagonal > sum of two sides forming a triangle, the triangle (and hence quadrilateral) cannot be constructed.

questions.text: In constructing parallelogram ABCD, we need:
questions.explanation: A parallelogram: opposite sides are equal, so 2 adjacent sides give all sides. Still need 1 angle to fix the shape. 2 sides + 1 angle = 3 measurements ‚Üí unique parallelogram.

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, if AB = 5 cm, BC = 6 cm, CD = 4 cm, DA = 3 cm and diagonal BD = 8 cm, which triangle is constructed using sides BD, DC and BC?
questions.explanation: Triangle using sides BD, DC and BC ‚Üí vertices B, D, C ‚Üí Triangle BCD.

questions.text: To construct a quadrilateral when given 3 sides and 2 diagonals, the method used is:

options.a: Construct both triangles formed by a diagonal simultaneously
options.b: Construct one triangle at a time using the two diagonals
options.c: Use the intersection of arcs from the two diagonals to locate vertices
options.d: Use angle bisectors of the given angles

questions.explanation: With 3 sides and 2 diagonals, the intersection of arcs drawn from the diagonal lengths helps locate the 4th vertex.

questions.text: In the figure, BEST is a quadrilateral with BE = 4.5 cm, ES = 6 cm, ST = 3.5 cm, BT = 4 cm and diagonal BS = 5 cm. In ‚Ė≥BES, which side is opposite to vertex B?
questions.explanation: In ‚Ė≥BES, the side opposite to vertex B is ES (the side not touching B).

questions.text: Which of the following quadrilaterals requires the least number of independent measurements to construct uniquely?
questions.explanation: Square: needs only 1 measurement (side). Rectangle: 2 measurements. Parallelogram: 3 measurements. General quadrilateral: 5 measurements.

questions.text: For constructing quadrilateral LAND with LA = 4 cm, AN = 4 cm, ND = 4 cm, DL = 4 cm and ‚ą†A = 75¬į. What type of quadrilateral is this most likely?
questions.explanation: All 4 sides equal (4 cm each) with one angle = 75¬į (not 90¬į) ‚Üí Rhombus (all sides equal but not all angles 90¬į).

questions.text: In the figure, quadrilateral PQRS is constructed with PQ = 5 cm, QR = 6 cm, RS = 7 cm, SP = 4 cm and diagonal PR = 6.5 cm. The total number of measurements used is:
questions.explanation: PQ, QR, RS, SP (4 sides) + PR (1 diagonal) = 5 measurements. This is the minimum for a unique general quadrilateral.

questions.text: In quadrilateral MORE, MO = 6 cm, OR = 4.5 cm, ME = 3.5 cm, ‚ą†M = 60¬į and ‚ą†O = 105¬į. The 4th angle ‚ą†E = ?
questions.explanation: First find ‚ą†R: in a quadrilateral, ‚ą†M+‚ą†O+‚ą†R+‚ą†E=360¬į. But ‚ą†R is not given ‚ÄĒ wait: this is the NCERT quadrilateral MORE with ‚ą†M=60¬į, ‚ą†O=105¬į, ‚ą†R=105¬į, ‚ą†E=90¬į. 60+105+105+90=360¬į ‚úď.

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, AB = 4.5 cm, BC = 5.5 cm, CD = 4 cm, AD = 6 cm and ‚ą†A = 80¬į. What is the step immediately after constructing ‚Ė≥ABD?

options.a: Draw arc of radius 5.5 cm from B
options.b: Draw arc of radius 4 cm from D
options.c: Both arcs from B and D intersect to give C

questions.explanation: After ‚Ė≥ABD is constructed, arcs of radius BC=5.5 cm from B and radius CD=4 cm from D are drawn. Their intersection gives vertex C.

questions.text: A quadrilateral JUMP has JU = 3.5 cm, UM = 4 cm, MP = 5 cm, PJ = 4 cm, JM = 6 cm. In ‚Ė≥JUM (using JU, UM, JM), check: is JM < JU + UM?
questions.explanation: JU+UM = 3.5+4 = 7.5 cm. JM = 6 cm. Since 6 < 7.5, the triangle inequality is satisfied. ‚Ė≥JUM can be constructed.

questions.text: To construct a parallelogram ABCD with AB = 6 cm, BC = 4.5 cm and ‚ą†B = 75¬į. What is ‚ą†A?
questions.explanation: In parallelogram, adjacent angles are supplementary. ‚ą†A = 180¬į ‚ąí ‚ą†B = 180¬į ‚ąí 75¬į = 105¬į.

questions.text: In the figure, quadrilateral PQRS is to be constructed with PQ = 4 cm, QR = 3 cm, diagonal PR = 5 cm, diagonal QS = 6.5 cm and RS = 5 cm. The diagonals PR and QS intersect at O. Which triangle is constructed first?
questions.explanation: ‚Ė≥PQR has all 3 sides known (PQ=4, QR=3, PR=5). 4¬≤+3¬≤=16+9=25=5¬≤ ‚Üí right triangle. This is constructed first.

questions.text: Construct rhombus ABCD with side 5 cm and diagonal AC = 8 cm. In ‚Ė≥ABC, what is the length of BC?
questions.explanation: All sides of rhombus are equal. BC = side = 5 cm.

questions.text: In the figure, quadrilateral OKAY is constructed with OK = 7 cm, KA = 5 cm, AY = 6 cm, OY = 3 cm and KY = 5.5 cm (diagonal). In ‚Ė≥OKY, which condition must be checked before construction?

options.a: OK + KY > OY and OY + KY > OK and OK + OY > KY

questions.explanation: Triangle inequality must hold for all three pairs: each side must be less than the sum of the other two. All three conditions must be checked.

questions.text: To construct rectangle ABCD with AB = 7 cm and BC = 5 cm, which angle is drawn first at vertex A?
questions.explanation: All angles in a rectangle = 90¬į. The first step after drawing AB is to construct a 90¬į angle at A (and at B) to draw the perpendicular sides.

questions.text: In the figure, ABCD is a quadrilateral with AB = 5 cm, ‚ą†A = 50¬į, ‚ą†B = 110¬į, AD = 4 cm and BC = 4.5 cm. What is ‚ą†C + ‚ą†D?
In constructing quadrilateral ABCD with AB = 4 cm, BC = 3 cm, ‚ą†A = 90¬į, ‚ą†B = 90¬į and CD = 2 cm, what type of quadrilateral is formed?
questions.explanation: ‚ą†A=90¬į and ‚ą†B=90¬į means AB is perpendicular to both AD and BC. So AD ‚ą• BC (both perpendicular to AB). Only one pair of parallel sides (AD and BC if they exist and are unequal), making it a right trapezium with two right angles.

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, AB = 6 cm, BC = 4 cm, CD = 4 cm, DA = 5 cm and ‚ą†B = 90¬į. After constructing ‚Ė≥ABC with the right angle at B, the arc from A of radius 5 cm and arc from C of radius 4 cm intersect to give D. If AD were 4.5 cm instead of 5 cm, and the arc from C of radius 4 cm is fixed, which of the following is true?

options.a: D would be closer to A
options.b: D would be farther from A
options.c: D would be the same point

questions.explanation: A shorter AD=4.5 cm (instead of 5 cm) means the arc from A has a smaller radius. The intersection with the fixed arc from C gives a point D that is closer to A.

questions.text: In the figure, diagonal AC divides quadrilateral ABCD into ‚Ė≥ABC and ‚Ė≥ACD. In ‚Ė≥ABC: AB=5, BC=6, AC=7. In ‚Ė≥ACD: AC=7, CD=8, DA=4. Verify if ‚Ė≥ABC can be constructed. Is AB + BC > AC?

options.c: Yes: but AC > AB + BC, so impossible

questions.explanation: AB+BC = 5+6=11 > AC=7 ‚úď. AC+BC = 7+6=13 > AB=5 ‚úď. AB+AC = 5+7=12 > BC=6 ‚úď. All triangle inequalities satisfied ‚ÄĒ ‚Ė≥ABC can be constructed.

questions.text: A quadrilateral ABCD has AB = BC = CD = DA = 6 cm and diagonal AC = 6 cm. What is the shape of ‚Ė≥ABC and what are all angles of the quadrilateral?

options.a: ‚Ė≥ABC is equilateral; all angles of ABCD = 60¬į
options.d: ‚Ė≥ABC is scalene; no information about angles

questions.explanation: AB=BC=AC=6 cm ‚Üí ‚Ė≥ABC is equilateral ‚Üí ‚ą†ABC=60¬į. Similarly ‚Ė≥ACD is equilateral ‚Üí ‚ą†ADC=60¬į. ‚ą†DAB = 360¬į-(60¬į+60¬į+60¬į+‚ą†DAB)... wait: ‚ą†B=60¬į, ‚ą†D=60¬į. ‚ą†A+‚ą†C=360¬į-120¬į=240¬į. By symmetry ‚ą†A=‚ą†C=120¬į. ‚úď

questions.text: In the figure, quadrilateral ABCD is constructed. AB = 3.5 cm, BC = 6.5 cm, ‚ą†B = 105¬į, ‚ą†C = 80¬į and CD = 5 cm. What is the step after constructing BC?

options.a: Draw ‚ą†CBX = 80¬į at C and cut CD = 5 cm
options.b: Draw ‚ą†CBY = 105¬į at B and cut AB = 3.5 cm

questions.explanation: After constructing BC, the next step is to construct ‚ą†BCD = 80¬į at C (using C as centre), then cut off CD = 5 cm on that ray. Then similarly construct ‚ą†ABC = 105¬į at B to locate A.

questions.text: A rectangle ABCD has AB = 8 cm and its diagonal AC = 10 cm. What is BC, and what is the area of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: In rectangle: AC¬≤ = AB¬≤ + BC¬≤. 100 = 64 + BC¬≤. BC¬≤ = 36. BC = 6 cm. Area = 8 √ó 6 = 48 cm¬≤.

questions.text: In the figure, a quadrilateral ABCD is constructed with AB = 4 cm, BC = 5 cm, CD = 6 cm, DA = 5 cm and ‚ą†B = 90¬į. In ‚Ė≥ABC with ‚ą†B = 90¬į, what is AC (the diagonal)?
questions.explanation: In ‚Ė≥ABC with ‚ą†B = 90¬į: AC¬≤ = AB¬≤ + BC¬≤ = 16 + 25 = 41. AC = ‚ąö41 cm.

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, AB = 4.5 cm, BC = 5.5 cm, AC = 7 cm (diagonal), CD = 4 cm and AD = 5 cm. What is the perimeter?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = AB + BC + CD + DA = 4.5 + 5.5 + 4 + 5 = 19 cm. (Diagonal is not a side.)

questions.text: In rhombus ABCD with side 6 cm and diagonal BD = 8 cm. What is half of diagonal AC?
questions.explanation: Diagonals of rhombus bisect at 90¬į. Half of BD = 4 cm. In right triangle: AO¬≤ = AB¬≤ ‚ąí BO¬≤ = 36 ‚ąí 16 = 20. AO = ‚ąö20 cm.

questions.text: Parallelogram ABCD has AB = 7 cm, BC = 5 cm and ‚ą†B = 60¬į. What is ‚ą†D?
questions.explanation: Opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal. ‚ą†D = ‚ą†B = 60¬į.

questions.text: In the figure, quadrilateral PQRS is constructed. PQ = 3 cm, QR = 4 cm, ‚ą†Q = 90¬į, RS = 6 cm and PS = 6 cm. What is PR (the diagonal)?
questions.explanation: In ‚Ė≥PQR with ‚ą†Q = 90¬į: PR¬≤ = PQ¬≤ + QR¬≤ = 9 + 16 = 25. PR = 5 cm.

questions.text: Square ABCD has perimeter 24 cm. What is the length of each diagonal?
questions.explanation: Side = 24/4 = 6 cm. Diagonal = side √ó ‚ąö2 = 6‚ąö2 cm.

questions.text: In quadrilateral ABCD, ‚ą†A = 75¬į, ‚ą†B = 110¬į, ‚ą†C = 95¬į. What is ‚ą†D?
questions.explanation: ‚ą†D = 360¬į ‚ąí ‚ą†A ‚ąí ‚ą†B ‚ąí ‚ą†C = 360¬į ‚ąí 75¬į ‚ąí 110¬į ‚ąí 95¬į = 80¬į.

questions.text: A quadrilateral ABCD is to be constructed. It is found that AB + BC < AC (diagonal). Which statement is true?

options.a: The construction is possible with adjustment
options.b: The quadrilateral can still be constructed
options.c: ‚Ė≥ABC cannot be formed ‚ÄĒ construction is impossible
options.d: Only the other triangle ACD can be constructed

questions.explanation: Triangle inequality: for a triangle to exist, every side must be less than the sum of the other two. If AB + BC < AC, then ‚Ė≥ABC cannot be formed and the quadrilateral cannot be constructed.

questions.text: In constructing parallelogram ABCD with AB = 5 cm, AD = 3 cm and ‚ą†DAB = 70¬į. What is CD?
questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, opposite sides are equal. CD = AB = 5 cm.

questions.text: In rectangle PQRS, PQ = 9 cm and diagonal PR = 15 cm. What is QR?
questions.explanation: In rectangle, ‚ą†Q = 90¬į. PR¬≤ = PQ¬≤ + QR¬≤. 225 = 81 + QR¬≤. QR¬≤ = 144. QR = 12 cm.

questions.text: For constructing quadrilateral ABCD with AB = 4 cm, BC = 3 cm, CD = 6 cm, DA = 7 cm and diagonal BD, which value of BD would make the construction impossible (triangle inequality fails in ‚Ė≥ABD)?
questions.explanation: In ‚Ė≥ABD: AB=4, DA=7. Triangle inequality: BD < AB+DA = 11. If BD=12 > 11, ‚Ė≥ABD cannot be formed. BD=11 makes it degenerate. BD=12 definitely fails.

cases.case_text: A land surveyor needs to map a quadrilateral plot ABCD. He measures: AB = 5 m, BC = 6 m, CD = 7 m, DA = 4 m and the diagonal AC = 8 m. He wants to draw an accurate scale diagram at 1 cm = 1 m.

subparts.text: In ‚Ė≥ABC (AB=5, BC=6, AC=8), check the triangle inequality: is AB + BC > AC?
subparts.explanation: 5+6=11 > 8 ‚úď. Also 5+8=13 > 6 ‚úď and 6+8=14 > 5 ‚úď. All triangle inequalities hold for ‚Ė≥ABC.

subparts.text: In ‚Ė≥ACD (AC=8, CD=7, DA=4), is the construction possible?

options.b: Yes: CD+DA = 11 > AC = 8, and all inequalities hold

subparts.explanation: CD+DA = 7+4=11 > AC=8 ‚úď. AC+DA = 8+4=12 > CD=7 ‚úď. AC+CD = 8+7=15 > DA=4 ‚úď. All valid ‚ÄĒ ‚Ė≥ACD can be constructed.

subparts.text: The perimeter of the plot ABCD is:

cases.case_text: A student is constructing quadrilateral WXYZ with WX = 4.5 cm, XY = 5 cm, YZ = 6 cm, ZW = 4 cm and diagonal WY = 7 cm. She constructs ‚Ė≥WXY first, then uses WY and ZW, YZ to locate Z.

subparts.text: In ‚Ė≥WXY (WX=4.5, XY=5, WY=7): is the largest side WY less than the sum of the other two?
After constructing ‚Ė≥WXY, Z is located by drawing arcs of radius ZW=4 cm from W and radius YZ=6 cm from Y. Which arc radius is drawn from W?
subparts.explanation: Z is connected to W by side ZW=4 cm. So the arc from W has radius = ZW = 4 cm.

subparts.text: What is the perimeter of quadrilateral WXYZ?
